_comment: Playbook schema for bOS Co-pilot. One .json file per feature in this folder. Files starting with _ are ignored by the app. Claude authors these from chat; you drop them in and the app renders them as step-by-step guidance pinned to your real object tree AND auto-grades each step against your .bos (Implemented / Partial / To-do). The .bos the tool reads is your system, so a green step is already in place - you do not re-verify it by hand. The app renders: n, title, action, target, detail, check, diff. A legacy 'verify' field is NOT displayed; use 'check' instead.
feature: Short feature name shown in the dropdown
summary: 1-3 sentence plain-language description of what this change does and the rule it implements.

steps.title: Short imperative step title
steps.action: navigate | add | set   (controls the colored action badge: navigate=go to a node, add=create a node, set=set a value/binding)
steps.target: Tasks\Exact\Node\Path   (a real node path; clicking the step highlights it yellow in the left tree)
steps.detail: Exactly what to click/do in the Configurator for this step.

ui._comment: Optional guided-walkthrough action(s) for the real ComfortClick apps. INSTRUCTIONAL ONLY - Hyperion guides the human step-by-step; the human performs every click, nothing is automated. May be a single object or an array of them (for a multi-click step). Rendered in the step as an app badge + location + action + expected result. Invalid entries (unknown app, or empty action) are dropped by the loader; a step with no valid entry simply shows no ui block. Keep optional so older playbooks stay valid.
ui.app: Configurator | Service | Client   (which real bOS app to act in)
ui.location: Where in that app's UI - the menu path or panel, e.g. 'Tasks > Kylvatten anläggning tak 84000-CM1'
ui.action: Exactly what to click, set, or type, e.g. 'Right-click > Add > Program; name it Stopp takpump vid läckage'
ui.verify: What the operator should see to confirm the step worked

check._comment: Makes the step auto-grade Implemented (all true) / Partial (some) / To-do (none). 'node' is read from your .bos; every predicate in 'all' is tested against it and shown with a checkmark/cross next to its 'label'.

all.label: an object whose path contains this string references the node (its Usages)
this node writes to an object whose path contains this string
(Program) a command contains this text; optional 'under' requires it nested under a command containing that string

diff._comment: Optional Before/After command-tree preview shown under the step. Mark new lines added:true and removed lines removed:true. Preview only - nothing is written to bOS. 'section' is 'commands' or 'triggers'.

highlight._comment: Optional. When the step is selected, the app navigates to 'node' and highlights the exact field ON the page, overlaying a before->after chip. May be a single object or an array of them. kind = setting (Modbus/settings row, field = setting key e.g. ReadCyclically) | command (Program command line, field = substring of the command text e.g. 'TEST TAKSTATUS.Value = True') | binding (theme tile / write target, field = substring of the bound object path). before/after render the struck old value and green new value; warn renders a red badge next to the row.
highlight.field: ReadCyclically  (setting key) | command-text substring | bound-object substring
